rulings.Case Title: ROBERT P. SHULTZ VS. AK STEEL CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Defendant United States Steel Corporation, Individually And As Successor To Oilwell, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 27, 2025, in Department 304, Line 1: 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication as Defendant United States Steel Corporation, Individually and as Successor-In-Interest to Oilwell, Inc., is GRANTED. Opposition filed. Reply filed. Plaintiff moves for summary adjudication as to six affirmative defenses asserted by Defendant Untied States Steel Corporation ("Defendant"). Plaintiff argues that Defendant has no evidence to support these affirmative defenses and has served factually devoid discovery responses. (Motion, 1.) Defendant opposes the motion, arguing that summary adjudication is improper because Plaintiff has not produced any admissible evidence and discovery is ongoing. (Opposition, 1.) The Court rules on each of the affirmative defenses as follows: " First, Fourth, Fifth, Seventh, Fourteenth, and Eighteenth Affirmative Defenses - Assumption of Risk; Contributory Negligence; Comparative Fault of Other Parties; Misuse; Government Contractor Defense; and Sophisticated User The motion for summary adjudication as to Defendant's First, Fourth, Fifth, Seventh, Fourteenth, and Eighteenth Affirmative Defenses is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling was e-served to all counsel. Transaction ID #75943355) The moving party shall lodge with the clerk in Department 304 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. Any party wishing to contest the tentative ruling must email contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing and state their intention to contest. If a hearing is requested, it will be on March 27, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. Attorneys may appear in person or remotely via zoom: Meeting ID 160 757 8308; Passcode: 485029. Face coverings are optional. The Court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law and Motion department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: Their name, CSR and telephone number, and their individual work email address. There will be only one official record. If the parties cannot agree, the Court will designate a qualified court reporter to provide the official transcript for the matter, and the party or parties will bear the cost. = (EPS/304)
